text: The only way to deal with an unfree world is to become so absolutely free that your very existence is an act of rebellion.
author: Albert Camus

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
author: Buddha

text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: One cannot step twice in the same river.
author: Heraclitus

text: Man is condemned to be free; because once thrown into the world, he is responsible for everything he does.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: The greatest wealth is to live content with little.
author: Plato

text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: The obstacle is the way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: What we achieve inwardly will change outer reality.
author: Plutarch

text: Knowing others is intelligence; knowing yourself is true wisdom.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Until you make the unconscious conscious, it will direct your life and you will call it fate.
author: Carl Jung

text: The soul becomes dyed with the colour of its thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Life must be understood backwards; but it must be lived forwards.
author: Soren Kierkegaard

text: Whoever fights monsters should see to it that in the process he does not become a monster.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The wound is the place where the Light enters you.
author: Rumi

text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Aristotle

text: Your vision will become clear only when you can look into your own heart.
author: Carl Jung

text: No man's knowledge here can go beyond his experience.
author: John Locke

text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The purpose of life is not to be happy. It is to be useful, to be honorable, to be compassionate.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Freedom is what you do with what's been done to you.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: Everything you can imagine is real.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: The privilege of a lifetime is to become who you truly are.
author: Carl Jung

text: Man is by nature a social animal.
author: Aristotle

text: The only thing I know is that I know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: When you realize nothing is lacking, the whole world belongs to you.
author: Lao Tzu

text: There is nothing either good or bad, but thinking makes it so.
author: William Shakespeare

text: The snake which cannot cast its skin has to die. As well the minds which are prevented from changing their opinions; they cease to be mind.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Happiness depends upon ourselves.
author: Aristotle

text: He who knows others is wise; he who knows himself is enlightened.
author: Lao Tzu

text: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a great deal of it.
author: Seneca

text: The cave you fear to enter holds the treasure you seek.
author: Joseph Campbell

text: We don't see things as they are, we see them as we are.
author: Anaïs Nin

text: To be yourself in a world that is constantly trying to make you something else is the greatest accomplishment.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: I think, therefore I am.
author: Rene Descartes

text: The world is a book and those who do not travel read only one page.
author: Augustine of Hippo

text: What does not kill me makes me stronger.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Every human being is a question, and the whole of life is the answer.
author: Martin Buber

text: The quieter you become, the more you can hear.
author: Ram Dass

text: One must still have chaos in oneself to be able to give birth to a dancing star.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Reality is merely an illusion, albeit a very persistent one.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The journey of a thousand miles begins with one step.
author: Lao Tzu

text: We are more often frightened than hurt; and we suffer more from imagination than from reality.
author: Seneca

text: Man's search for meaning is the primary motivation in his life.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: Those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it.
author: George Santayana

text: When the student is ready, the teacher will appear.
author: Buddhist Proverb

text: A smooth sea never made a skilled sailor.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

text: The function of prayer is not to influence God, but rather to change the nature of the one who prays.
author: Soren Kierkegaard

text: The mind is its own place, and in itself can make a heaven of hell, a hell of heaven.
author: John Milton

text: Do not dwell in the past, do not dream of the future, concentrate the mind on the present moment.
author: Buddha

text: He who has overcome his fears will truly be free.
author: Aristotle

text: In every walk with nature one receives far more than he seeks.
author: John Muir

text: To live is to suffer, to survive is to find some meaning in the suffering.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The measure of intelligence is the ability to change.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Between stimulus and response there is a space. In that space is our power to choose our response.
author: Viktor Frankl

text: What you resist, persists.
author: Carl Jung

text: The only impossible journey is the one you never begin.
author: Tony Robbins

text: To see a world in a grain of sand and a heaven in a wild flower, hold infinity in the palm of your hand and eternity in an hour.
author: William Blake

text: Life is not a problem to be solved, but a reality to be experienced.
author: Soren Kierkegaard

text: The cosmos is within us. We are made of star-stuff. We are a way for the universe to know itself.
author: Carl Sagan

text: I can control my destiny, but not my fate. Destiny means there are opportunities to choose. Fate is that which is given.
author: Carl Jung

text: The art of being wise is the art of knowing what to overlook.
author: William James

text: It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it.
author: Aristotle

text: The greatest enemy of knowledge is not ignorance, it is the illusion of knowledge.
author: Daniel J. Boorstin

text: Truth is not something outside to be discovered, it is something inside to be realized.
author: Osho

text: Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

text: We are not troubled by things, but by the opinion which we have of things.
author: Epictetus

text: To thine own self be true.
author: William Shakespeare

text: The person who says it cannot be done should not interrupt the person who is doing it.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Everything has beauty, but not everyone sees it.
author: Confucius

text: Science without religion is lame, religion without science is blind.
author: Albert Einstein

text: The secret of health for both mind and body is not to mourn for the past, not to worry about the future, but to live the present moment wisely and earnestly.
author: Buddha

text: God is dead. God remains dead. And we have killed him.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The heart has its reasons which reason knows not.
author: Blaise Pascal

text: We are all in the gutter, but some of us are looking at the stars.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: I would rather die on my feet than live on my knees.
author: Euripides

text: He who thinks little errs much.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: Imagination is more important than knowledge.
author: Albert Einstein

text: A man who dares to waste one hour of time has not discovered the value of life.
author: Charles Darwin

text: The first duty of love is to listen.
author: Paul Tillich

text: Without music, life would be a mistake.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The sage does not hoard. The more he helps others, the more he benefits himself.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Man is nothing else but what he makes of himself.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: Our life is what our thoughts make it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: When I let go of what I am, I become what I might be.
author: Lao Tzu

text: The bravest sight in the world is a man fighting against destiny.
author: Albert Camus

text: People are disturbed not by things, but by the views which they take of them.
author: Epictetus

text: The whole is more than the sum of its parts.
author: Aristotle

text: To do as one would be done by, and to love one's neighbor as oneself, constitute the ideal perfection of utilitarian morality.
author: John Stuart Mill

text: He who learns must suffer. And even in our sleep pain that cannot forget falls drop by drop upon the heart.
author: Aeschylus

text: The meaning of life is to find your gift. The purpose of life is to give it away.
author: Pablo Picasso

text: Be here now.
author: Ram Dass

text: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow is our doubts of today.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

text: If you are depressed you are living in the past. If you are anxious you are living in the future. If you are at peace you are living in the present.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Courage is not the absence of fear, but the triumph over it.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: Your task is not to seek for love, but merely to seek and find all the barriers within yourself that you have built against it.
author: Rumi

text: There is but one truly serious philosophical problem, and that is suicide.
author: Albert Camus

text: The master has failed more times than the beginner has even tried.
author: Stephen McCranie

text: Nothing is permanent in this wicked world — not even our troubles.
author: Charlie Chaplin

text: The individual has always had to struggle to keep from being overwhelmed by the tribe.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Do not go where the path may lead, go instead where there is no path and leave a trail.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: We must be willing to let go of the life we planned so as to have the life that is waiting for us.
author: Joseph Campbell

text: Wheresoever you go, go with all your heart.
author: Confucius

text: The irrationality of a thing is no argument against its existence, rather a condition of it.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Man is not worried by real problems so much as by his imagined anxieties about real problems.
author: Epictetus

text: The world as we have created it is a process of our thinking. It cannot be changed without changing our thinking.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Life must be understood backwards; but it must be lived forwards.
author: Soren Kierkegaard

text: Anyone who has never made a mistake has never tried anything new.
author: Albert Einstein

text: A gem cannot be polished without friction, nor a man perfected without trials.
author: Seneca

text: The beginning of wisdom is the definition of terms.
author: Socrates

text: Real knowledge is to know the extent of one's ignorance.
author: Confucius

text: He who conquers himself is the mightiest warrior.
author: Confucius

text: We become just by performing just actions, temperate by performing temperate actions, brave by performing brave actions.
author: Aristotle

text: There are only two ways to live your life. One is as though nothing is a miracle. The other is as though everything is a miracle.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Loneliness does not come from having no people about one, but from being unable to communicate the things that seem important to oneself.
author: Carl Jung

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: The greatest glory in living lies not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: What we think, we become.
author: Buddha

text: You must be the change you wish to see in the world.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: The only person you are destined to become is the person you decide to be.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: The mind is not a vessel to be filled, but a fire to be kindled.
author: Plutarch

text: If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.
author: African Proverb

text: I am not what happened to me, I am what I choose to become.
author: Carl Jung

text: Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated.
author: Confucius

text: The pessimist complains about the wind; the optimist expects it to change; the realist adjusts the sails.
author: William Arthur Ward

text: When you reach the end of your rope, tie a knot in it and hang on.
author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

text: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: An unexamined life is not worth living, but a life examined to excess is not worth examining.
author: Mark Twain

text: There is a voice that doesn't use words. Listen.
author: Rumi

text: The deeper that sorrow carves into your being, the more joy you can contain.
author: Kahlil Gibran

text: Our deepest fear is not that we are inadequate. Our deepest fear is that we are powerful beyond measure.
author: Marianne Williamson

text: Every wall is a door.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: You cannot step into the same river twice, for other waters are continually flowing in.
author: Heraclitus

text: All that we are is the result of what we have thought.
author: Buddha

text: The greatest deception men suffer is from their own opinions.
author: Leonardo da Vinci

text: In the depth of winter, I finally learned that within me there lay an invincible summer.
author: Albert Camus

text: Chaos is merely order waiting to be deciphered.
author: Jose Saramago

text: The door you open to give love is the very door through which love arrives.
author: Alan Watts

text: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Who looks outside, dreams; who looks inside, awakes.
author: Carl Jung

text: If you are going through hell, keep going.
author: Winston Churchill

text: The flame that burns twice as bright burns half as long.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Life is a series of natural and spontaneous changes. Don't resist them. Let reality be reality.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Before enlightenment, chop wood, carry water. After enlightenment, chop wood, carry water.
author: Zen Proverb

text: What you get by achieving your goals is not as important as what you become by achieving your goals.
author: Zig Ziglar

text: The seed does not become a tree in a day. Neither does a person become wise in a day.
author: Indian Proverb
